recentDeals: [{"company":"Hidden Level","category":"Drone Detection / Defense Tech","year":2024},{"company":"Pathos AI","category":"AI Drug Development","year":2024},{"company":"Tempus","category":"AI Healthcare / IPO","year":2024},{"company":"CAVA","category":"Food Tech / IPO","year":2023}]
thesis: "Third Wave" — technology disrupting healthcare, education, food, finance, and transportation. Core belief that great companies can start anywhere, not just coastal hubs. Midwest is a primary target geography.
whyFit: The strongest Columbus connection of any firm. Rise of the Rest invested in SafeChain (Columbus), Steve Case's bus tour ranked Columbus #1 Rising City. Anduril's $1B Ohio factory. JD Vance was ROTR's original managing partner. Broadest sector coverage overlapping nearly all Collective AI departments.
aiRelevanceExplanation: Active AI portfolio including Pathos AI ($62M, drug development), Hidden Level (drone detection AI), and Tempus (AI healthcare, IPO'd 2024). Revolution Growth and Ventures both deploy into AI-native companies across multiple verticals.
outreachAngle: Leverage Columbus ecosystem connection — SafeChain, ROTR bus tour history, Anduril Ohio presence. Position Collective AI as the Columbus venture studio validating the Third Wave thesis. Emphasize multi-department AI infrastructure alignment.
recommendedNextStep: Warm intro via Columbus startup ecosystem (Rev1 Ventures, ROTR alumni network). Prepare deck emphasizing Third Wave alignment and Columbus AI ecosystem.
warmIntro: Columbus startup ecosystem (Rev1 Ventures), ROTR alumni network, JD Vance connection

recentDeals: [{"company":"Visana Health","category":"Virtual Women's Healthcare","year":2024},{"company":"Ediphi","category":"Hospitality Training","year":2024},{"company":"Starling Medical","category":"Patient Monitoring","year":2024},{"company":"Anonymous Health","category":"Addiction Outpatient Care","year":2024}]
thesis: "Smart Capital From Experienced Entrepreneurs" — coaching founders from diverse backgrounds building industry-changing companies in digital health, InsurTech, ecommerce, cybersecurity, and enterprise SaaS.
whyFit: Has a physical office in Columbus, OH. Already invested in two Columbus startups (TNEDICCA, MentorcliQ). Co-invested with Rev1 Ventures and Grange Insurance. Partners share Rally Health/UnitedHealth Group operating backgrounds — deep HealthTech expertise.
aiRelevanceExplanation: While not an AI-first fund, portfolio companies like Visana Health and Starling Medical leverage AI for healthcare delivery. Digital health focus aligns with AI-powered diagnostic and care optimization trends.
outreachAngle: Local Columbus presence — easiest path to meeting. Highlight Vital Helix department's health-tech AI capabilities. Reference co-investment history with Rev1 Ventures.
recommendedNextStep: Direct outreach via Columbus office. Arrange in-person meeting. Emphasize shared Columbus ecosystem commitment and HealthTech AI alignment.
warmIntro: Rev1 Ventures, Grange Insurance, Columbus startup community

thesis: "Durable founders who have felt the pain" — early-revenue B2B SaaS with AI integration. Strong High Alpha (Indianapolis venture studio) relationship.
whyFit: Josh Kline is described as 'tip of spear sales leader for emerging tech in the Ohio Valley.' Frequently co-invests with Allos Ventures (Columbus, OH-based) across 4+ shared deals. Adjacent geography with strong Ohio network.
aiRelevanceExplanation: Heavy AI portfolio: Backstroke (AI marketing), Stellar (AI business solutions — acquired for first exit), Hyphenate (AI sales proposals). The firm is explicitly investing in AI-integrated B2B SaaS.
outreachAngle: Leverage Allos Ventures co-investment network for warm introduction. Position Collective AI as the venture studio model that validates the 'durable founders' thesis. Highlight ZenFlow AI infrastructure.
recommendedNextStep: Warm intro through Allos Ventures (Columbus). Present B2B SaaS AI thesis alignment and venture studio model.
warmIntro: Allos Ventures (Columbus, OH), High Alpha network

portfolioHighlights: ["40+ companies","48% women CEOs","66% people of color","LPs include PayPal Ventures & Pivotal Ventures"]
thesis: "Investing in the next generation of badass founders" from backgrounds not well understood by traditional investors. Core sectors: FinTech, HealthTech, EdTech, Future of Work.
whyFit: Explicit Midwest focus — created to fill the gap in markets 'particularly outside coastal hubs and in the Midwest.' Fund II at $52M shows institutional scale. Among the highest sector overlap with Collective AI portfolio, covering 7+ departments.
aiRelevanceExplanation: Active AI deployments: Ciphero AI Technologies (cybersecurity), Optimal AI (code review). Pre-seed focus means they invest at the earliest AI company stages — ideal for ZenFlow and Binary Loom ventures.
outreachAngle: Emphasize Midwest location and diverse founding team. Highlight venture studio model creating opportunities for underrepresented founders. Position as exactly the type of non-coastal innovation Chingona was built to fund.
recommendedNextStep: Cold outreach to Samara — emphasize Midwest thesis alignment and diverse founder commitment. Reference LP alignment (PayPal Ventures, Pivotal Ventures).
warmIntro: Midwest VC network, possible Chicago ecosystem connections

thesis: Vertical AI + Embedded Finance. 'We back software that saves time and moves money.' Capital desert thesis about the Midwest being underserved by VC.
whyFit: Minneapolis HQ with Cincinnati listed as a target city — Ohio is within geographic scope. Strong 'capital desert' thesis aligns with Columbus. Fund II at $41M with venture studio operations. Sharpened focus on Vertical AI and Embedded Finance perfectly maps to ZenFlow and Quantum Ledger.
aiRelevanceExplanation: Thesis explicitly sharpened to Vertical AI. Portfolio includes Flywheel.io (vertical AI), EnsoData (AI healthcare), and Nested Knowledge (AI research). Every new investment is evaluated through an AI lens.
outreachAngle: Highlight Columbus as part of the Midwest capital desert they target. Emphasize Collective AI as a venture studio producing both vertical AI and embedded finance companies — their exact thesis.
recommendedNextStep: Research Cincinnati portfolio for warm intro path. Cold outreach emphasizing Midwest thesis and vertical AI / embedded finance alignment.
warmIntro: Midwest VC ecosystem, possible Cincinnati connections

thesis: Hyper-local Kansas City fund — companies must have a strategic connection to western Missouri or Kansas. Co-investment model requiring another institutional VC in the round.
whyFit: Strictly KC metro mandate — not investing in Ohio. However, relevant as a Midwest VC ecosystem model and potential co-investor network reference. Broad sector coverage including cybersecurity, FinTech, and SportsTech.
aiRelevanceExplanation: Sailes (AI sales automation) in portfolio. Invary and SpiderOak represent AI-adjacent cybersecurity. Limited pure AI focus but growing interest through defense-tech lens.
outreachAngle: Co-investor network play — KCRise often co-invests with other Midwest funds. Could provide introductions to their LP and co-investor network. Geographic mandate limits direct investment.
recommendedNextStep: Lower priority due to KC-only mandate. Monitor for geographic expansion or co-investment opportunities with other Midwest VCs.
warmIntro: Midwest VC ecosystem connections

thesis: "Digital marketplaces bringing legacy industries online" — manufacturing, logistics, construction, supply chain. Active Substack covers marketplace economics and AI-powered marketplaces.
whyFit: Chicago HQ, Midwest-friendly. Brand-new fund (closed Feb 2026 at $50M) — actively deploying. Industrial/supply chain thesis fits Ohio's manufacturing economy. Ex-Pritzker Group partners bring institutional quality.
aiRelevanceExplanation: Thesis includes AI-powered marketplaces. Substack content focuses on AI transformation of industrial sectors. Less pure AI focus, more AI-as-enabler for marketplace models.
outreachAngle: Highlight Vector Shift department's logistics/mobility AI capabilities. Position Ohio's industrial economy as the natural market for SNAK's marketplace thesis. Reference Midwest proximity advantage.
recommendedNextStep: Cold outreach emphasizing marketplace + AI thesis. Highlight Columbus/Ohio industrial economy alignment and Midwest location.
warmIntro: Pritzker Group alumni network, Chicago VC ecosystem

recentDeals: [{"company":"KeyCare","category":"Epic-based Virtual Care","year":2024},{"company":"Minset","category":"Agentic AI Revenue Cycle","year":2025},{"company":"Dyania Health","category":"AI Chart Review","year":2024},{"company":"Cylerity","category":"Healthcare Payments","year":2024},{"company":"Sober Sidekick","category":"Addiction Recovery","year":2024}]
thesis: "Making healthcare safer, more efficient, and more affordable" through digital health solutions. Epic ecosystem adjacency is the core strategic advantage.
whyFit: Madison WI HQ — strong Midwest healthcare network. Dyania Health deployed at Cleveland Clinic (Ohio connection). $137M raised since 2015. Portfolio unicorn Medable. Healthcare-only focus aligns precisely with Vital Helix department.
aiRelevanceExplanation: Actively investing in AI healthcare: Minset (agentic AI for revenue cycle), Dyania Health (AI chart review). Healthcare AI is the firm's primary growth vector. 36 companies valued at $4.3B combined.
outreachAngle: Lead with Vital Helix department's health-tech AI capabilities. Reference Cleveland Clinic deployment as Ohio proximity. Emphasize AI-native approach to healthcare innovation.
recommendedNextStep: Research Cleveland Clinic / Dyania Health connection for warm intro path. Prepare Vital Helix-focused pitch deck.
warmIntro: Cleveland Clinic network, Epic Systems ecosystem, Midwest healthcare network

thesis: "AI is underhyped." Data-centric moats — proprietary data, economies of scale, network effects. 10% unicorn rate vs. 1% industry average. Never voted a founder out.
whyFit: One of the strongest thesis-fit investors on the list. ~$1B AUM, 12 unicorns, 82 exits. 'AI is underhyped' thesis perfectly aligns with Collective AI's AI-native approach. Broadest sector overlap covers 6+ departments. Fund V at $175M actively deploying.
aiRelevanceExplanation: Core thesis: 'AI is underhyped.' Recent AI investments: Supermemory (AI memory API), Memories AI, Plenful (AI healthcare), Together AI (unicorn). Data-centric moat thesis aligns with ZenFlow's AI infrastructure approach.
outreachAngle: Lead with AI thesis alignment — 'AI is underhyped' maps to Collective AI's mission. Emphasize data moat potential across venture studio portfolio. Reference 10% unicorn rate aspiration.
recommendedNextStep: Research warm intro paths through portfolio company networks. Prepare AI-thesis-centric deck emphasizing data moats and unicorn potential.
warmIntro: Portfolio company network (Robinhood, Flexport alumni)

thesis: "First to believe in founders building what doesn't yet exist." Contrarian, conviction-driven. 75% of Q4 2024 new investments were AI-native. 60+ repeat founders funded.
whyFit: Largest fund on the list at $3.8B+ AUM with 390+ companies. 75% of Q4 2024 deals are AI-native — highest AI commitment rate. Notable exits include Peloton, Ring, Fitbit, Duo Security, HashiCorp. Contrarian thesis fits non-coastal venture studio model.
aiRelevanceExplanation: 75% of Q4 2024 new investments were AI-native. Recent: Tenkara (AI manufacturing agents), Sailplane (AI cognitive freedom), Moneta Health (AI healthcare), WebSim (AI content generation). AI is now the dominant investment theme.
outreachAngle: Position Collective AI as exactly the type of contrarian, conviction-driven venture the firm was built to fund. Emphasize 75% AI-native alignment. Highlight venture studio as a novel approach they haven't backed yet.
recommendedNextStep: Warm intro through portfolio founder network. Prepare conviction-driven pitch emphasizing AI-native venture studio model.
warmIntro: Portfolio founder network, Kevin Rose (partner) social presence

portfolioHighlights: ["The Trade Desk (IPO)","TaxJar (acquired by Stripe)","MNTN ($2B IPO May 2025)","Vanta","9 unicorns"]
thesis: "Lead early, stay late." AI rewiring every workflow. ~80–85% Series A graduation rate (4x industry average). 100% B2B software focus.
whyFit: 100% B2B software focus with 9 unicorns and $18B+ exit value. Brett Queener (former Salesforce SVP) is an active thought leader on 'Agentic AI and the future of ARR.' Fund IV at $245M. Leads 95% of rounds with board seat — high conviction approach.
aiRelevanceExplanation: Thesis: 'AI rewiring every workflow.' Recent AI portfolio: Risotto (AI IT), Octave (AI marketing), Supio (AI legal), Topline Pro (gen AI for SMB). Brett Queener actively publishes on agentic AI. 80-85% Series A graduation rate means strong portfolio support.
outreachAngle: Lead with B2B AI thesis alignment. Reference Brett Queener's agentic AI thinking. Highlight venture studio model as a systematic approach to the 'AI rewiring every workflow' thesis.
recommendedNextStep: Engage Brett Queener through thought leadership content on agentic AI. Prepare B2B-focused pitch deck.
warmIntro: Brett Queener's social/thought leadership network, Salesforce alumni

thesis: "Backing Bold Founders with a Vision Worth Fighting For." Consumer software tapping new behavior. 16% of portfolio at $500M+ exit/valuation (10x industry average).
whyFit: Jim Scheinman literally named Zoom and was the earliest investor. 7 unicorns across ~50 investments — exceptional hit rate. Sara Thomas (partner) is Xavier University graduate (Cincinnati, OH connection). Perplexity and xAI in recent portfolio show cutting-edge AI conviction.
aiRelevanceExplanation: Recent AI portfolio includes Perplexity (AI answer engine, unicorn), xAI (Elon Musk's AI), The Bot Company (Kyle Vogt's robotics), Lutra AI (workflow automation), and Medeloop (AI clinical research). Consumer AI is #1 investment focus.

portfolioHighlights: ["3 companies on CB Insights Top 100 AI 2024","Latent Labs ($50M)","Variational AI","Accipiter Biosciences"]
thesis: "AI will disrupt every industry." 100% AI/ML-focused fund. Invests along the 'Augmentation to Automation Continuum.' Three pillars: augmentation-to-automation, computational discovery, and underlying AI shifts.
whyFit: One of the purest AI-only vehicles — 100% AI/ML focus. Three portfolio companies on CB Insights Top 100 AI 2024. Co-invested alongside Columbus Venture Partners in Accipiter Biosciences (tangential Columbus connection). Highest AI score on this list.
aiRelevanceExplanation: 100% AI/ML-focused fund — every investment is AI-native. Recent: Aisy (AI cybersecurity), AgileRL (reinforcement learning), Accipiter Biosciences (AI protein design), Simular (AI personal agent), Clarity (deepfake detection). Pure-play AI thesis.
outreachAngle: Lead with Accipiter Biosciences co-investment with Columbus Venture Partners as warm intro. Emphasize ZenFlow department's AI infrastructure thesis. Position Collective AI as an AI-native venture studio — perfect thesis match.
recommendedNextStep: Warm intro through Columbus Venture Partners (Accipiter co-investment). Prepare pure-AI thesis pitch emphasizing ZenFlow infrastructure.
warmIntro: Columbus Venture Partners (co-invested in Accipiter Biosciences)

thesis: "Technical differentiation" at seed stage. Invests where two disciplines collide (AI × transportation, bio × engineering). Three domains: software, life sciences, deep tech. PhD-heavy team with 150+ patents.
whyFit: $150M AUM deep tech fund. PhD-heavy team with 150+ patents — uniquely positioned to evaluate technical founders. 'Invests where two disciplines collide' aligns with Collective AI's cross-department approach. Notable exits to Atlassian and Datadog.
aiRelevanceExplanation: AI investments: Ruli.ai (legal AI), Synfini (AI drug development), d-Matrix (AI inference chips). Technical differentiation thesis values deep AI infrastructure — aligns with ZenFlow and Binary Loom approaches.
outreachAngle: Emphasize technical depth of AI infrastructure across departments. Highlight interdisciplinary collision thesis — venture studio model creates exactly these intersections. Reference deep tech portfolio exits.
recommendedNextStep: Technical deep-dive pitch emphasizing AI infrastructure and cross-discipline innovation. Lower priority due to no Midwest connection.
warmIntro: Deep tech founder network, academic connections

thesis: "Investing in Extraordinary Leadership" — leadership prevails. Industry-agnostic but defense/dual-use tech, AI, and cybersecurity are sweet spots. ~70% of deployments to military veteran founders.
whyFit: 112+ investments, 8 unicorns, 35+ acquisitions. Historical portfolio includes Slack, SpaceX, ID.me, Robinhood. West Point founders bring discipline and conviction. AI + cybersecurity + defense tech sweet spots align with Obsidian Arc and ZenFlow.
aiRelevanceExplanation: Active AI portfolio: Gallatin AI (military logistics AI), SGNL.ai (authorization AI), Apptronik (humanoid robots). Defense/AI intersection is growing. Industry-agnostic approach means AI thesis fit matters more than geography.
outreachAngle: If team has veteran connections, lead with that. Otherwise, emphasize leadership-driven thesis and AI/cybersecurity alignment. Reference Obsidian Arc department's defense-adjacent capabilities.
recommendedNextStep: Assess veteran founder connections on team. If present, prioritize outreach. Otherwise, standard AI/cybersecurity pitch.
warmIntro: Military/veteran founder network, defense tech ecosystem

thesis: "Founders-Turned Investors" backing AI and software modernizing outdated business workflows in 'nonobvious geographies and industries.'
whyFit: Explicitly invests in 'nonobvious geographies' — conceptually open to Midwest founders. 63 investments show institutional scale. Thesis about modernizing outdated workflows with AI aligns with Collective AI's cross-industry approach.
aiRelevanceExplanation: AI-focused thesis: modernizing outdated business workflows with AI and software. TriStar AI in portfolio. OneRail uses AI for last-mile logistics optimization. Growing AI focus across portfolio.
outreachAngle: Position Columbus as the definition of a 'nonobvious geography.' Emphasize venture studio model modernizing legacy industries with AI — exact thesis match. Highlight logistics and PropTech alignment.

portfolioHighlights: ["Claims 2 unicorns from 2021 vintage","Screens 9,000-12,000 teams/year","Uses 6 proprietary AI agents"]
thesis: "90% of what matters at seed is the founders." Uses proprietary AI agents including 'DaVinci' (RAG with 200K+ pages of VC knowledge) and personality analysis across 53 traits. AI-native fund operations.
whyFit: AI-native fund that uses proprietary AI agents to source and evaluate — unique operational thesis. Small check size ($250K-$500K) as non-lead co-investor. Requires a 'signal VC' from their top-50 list in the round. Interesting for co-investment but limited direct value.
aiRelevanceExplanation: The fund itself is AI-native — uses 6 proprietary AI agents including DaVinci (RAG system). This is interesting from a thesis alignment perspective but the fund's own AI operations are more notable than portfolio AI focus.
outreachAngle: Interesting as an AI-native fund operator. Could be valuable for network effects and co-investment alongside a lead VC. Small checks limit financial impact.
recommendedNextStep: Low priority as standalone — small non-lead checks require a signal VC lead. Consider as co-investor once a lead VC is secured.
warmIntro: Top-50 signal VC network

thesis: Breakthrough physical/industrial technology in underserved markets. Does NOT invest in human therapeutics, medical devices, or social media. Emphasizes diverse founders and sustainable impact.
whyFit: Deep tech/hard tech seed fund — one of the only Southeast VCs with this thesis. Sole decision-maker (Justin Wright-Eakes) means faster deal velocity. $50M+ deployed across ~20 companies shows selectivity. AI + AgriTech + cybersecurity alignment.
aiRelevanceExplanation: AI portfolio: Lemurian Labs (AI acceleration software), Targan (AI/robotics for poultry, 10x revenue growth), FortifyData (cybersecurity). Physical/industrial AI thesis aligns with Animus Prime and Gaia Synthesis departments.
outreachAngle: Emphasize physical/industrial AI applications. Highlight Gaia Synthesis and Animus Prime departments. Position as breakthrough technology in underserved markets — exact thesis match.
recommendedNextStep: Cold outreach to sole GP. Prepare deep tech pitch emphasizing physical/industrial AI and sustainable impact.
